destinations.evidence: TxAsyncCommitment carries ttl but no commit_block field or constructor argument.
destinations.snippet:
pub struct TxAsyncCommitment {
    ...
    pub ttl: u64,
    pub executor_address: Address,
    pub commitment_validator: Address,
}
...
pub fn new(..., ttl: u64, executor_address: Address, commitment_validator: Address) -> Self

destinations.evidence: Payload addJob calldata includes ttl and parent context fields but no commit_block argument.
destinations.snippet:
function addJob(... uint256 ttl, bytes32 prevHash, uint256 prevNum, uint256 prevTime, address sender, address commitVal, ...)
...
ttl: U256::from(ttl),
prevNum: U256::from(parent_number)

destinations.evidence: Canonical transaction-pool processing derives commit_block from the canonical block containing the async commitment and computes contract expiry as commit_block + ttl.

equivalence_argument: Mapped across alloy and the port: the commitment transaction/ABI does not carry commit_block, and pool lifecycle metadata obtains commit_block from canonical inclusion block number plus ttl-derived expiry.
rationale: The two candidates state the same durable contract between Reth and AsyncJobTracker after commit_block was removed from the transaction and ABI.
statement: Async commitment transactions and AsyncJobTracker.addJob calldata must not include a user-supplied commit_block; job timing is derived from inclusion block.number and ttl, and transaction-pool metadata derives commit_block from finalized expiry data rather than from the commitment transaction.
suggested_falsification_tests: Compile-fail or serialization test proving TxAsyncCommitment cannot encode a commit_block field.
Canonical block-processing test that mines an async commitment at block N with ttl T and asserts metadata.commit_block == N and contract_expiry_block == N + T.
summary: Async commit block is inclusion-derived in the port/dependency code.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Mine a commitment at a known block and assert pool metadata derives commit_block from the canonical block, not from transaction calldata.
title: Async commit block is inclusion derived
